mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, compressing required returns and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions, triggering immediate equity risk-off flows and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when unemployment remains below 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting corporate revenue growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in retail sales and housing starts fails to produce positive 60-day returns in at least 60% of historical occurrences, or interaction term loses significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle indicates resilient supply-side capacity that outpaces rate hike headwinds, driving short-term positive S&P 500 returns as earnings expectations rise.
failure_condition: Positive industrial production growth yields negative 5-day forward returns in over 50% of observations during tightening periods, or the signal reverses direction when FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index levels above 260 indicate entrenched inflationary pressures that force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, increasing equity discount rates and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in more than 60% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 260, or the negative correlation breaks down when real yields decline.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms a strengthening labor market that boosts consumer spending and corporate profitability, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising payrolls and falling unemployment fails to predict positive 60-day returns in at least 55% of cases, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth rates above trend directly translate to higher corporate earnings forecasts, creating immediate positive momentum in equity markets and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: GDP growth above 2.0% annualized fails to produce positive 5-day forward returns in more than 50% of release windows, or the signal shows zero predictive IC over rolling 2-year periods.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive months of declining industrial production signal a contracting manufacturing sector that reduces business investment and earnings growth, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in over 65% of instances following two consecutive months of industrial production declines, or the signal loses predictive power when inventory levels are rising.
